data.title: GRAIL submits FDA application for Galleri® Multi-Cancer Early Detection Test, advancing early detection, addressing unmet needs.
data.description: A significant development in cancer screening has been achieved with the submission to the FDA, marking a crucial milestone in advancing early cancer detection. The submission was made by GRAIL, Inc. (Nasdaq: GRAL), a healthcare company based in Menlo Park, California. Their primary goal is to detect cancer at an early stage when it is most treatable. This news comes on January 29, 2026, emphasizing the company's commitment to addressing unmet needs in cancer screening and potentially revolutionizing the field.
French equities were nearly flat with the CAC 40 up 0.03%, as Spain’s IBEX fell 0.17%.
The FTSE 100 in the UK gained 0.16%, whereas Germany’s DAX slid 2.13%.
Starbucks projects consolidated net revenue to grow at 5% or higher through 2028.
Europe’s STOXX 600 slipped 0.22%.
The Euro Stoxx index fell 0.69%, while euro zone blue chips declined 0.75%.

data.title: Students with dyslexia showing atypical gains in reading skills per NWEA MAP®, STAR®, and other assessments.
data.description: A recent report from NOW! Programs® in Jacksonville, Florida, indicates positive progress in reading and language skills. The results from NWEA MAP®, STAR®, aReading, AUTOreading, and WIST show noticeable improvements. The report was released by Dr. Tim Conway, Clinical Director of The Morris Center and CEO of NOW! Programs®, on January 29, 2026. The specific data and exact numbers detailing these gains were not included in the announcement.

data.title: FireFly Metals is conducting economic studies to benefit from a strong copper market outlook (ASX | TSX: FFM).
data.description: FireFly Metals, traded on ASX and TSX under symbol FFM, is strategically positioned to benefit from the positive forecast of copper prices. The company is currently conducting economic studies that aim to enhance its performance in this lucrative market. With copper prices on the rise, FireFly Metals seeks to maximize its potential and deliver promising results to investors.

data.title: Rio Tinto and Chalco acquire 68.596% share in CBA from Votorantim for R$10.50 per share, in joint venture with 33% and 67% ownership respectively.
data.description: In São Paulo, Brazil, Rio Tinto and Aluminum Corporation of China Limited (Chalco) have finalized a deal with Votorantim to purchase Votorantim's majority 68.596% stake in Companhia Brasileira de Alumínio (CBA). This agreement, valued at R$10.50 per share in CBA, will be executed through a joint venture where Rio Tinto will hold a 33% ownership and Chalco will have a 67% stake. The transaction marks a significant premium for the shareholders.

data.title: Dow Jones futures fall, software stocks dive but key indexes rebound. Apple and Sandisk rise after strong earnings.
data.description: Tech stocks took a hit on Thursday, but major stock market indexes managed to bounce back above critical benchmarks. Google and Nvidia were noted to be in favorable buying zones, while Apple and Sandisk saw late increases in response to their earnings reports. Dow Jones futures experienced a decline, although Apple's stock price saw an increase and Tesla's shares surged. Sandisk also witnessed a significant rise after the market maintained crucial levels.
